metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent PostgreSQL databases (main app DB + ToolJet DB), exposes CRUD operations on business entities (apps, data sources, organizations, users), and manages user-specific state with session/JWT authentication. Classified as stateful-crud.

classification_consistency_check.reason: Score 2.23 yields V5 Needs Work but 1 High findings yield V6 Pilot-Ready. Review severity mapping.

top_gaps.title: Auto-Scaling
top_gaps.gap: No auto-scaling configured in IaC; Helm HPA defaults to max=1
top_gaps.impact: Cannot respond to traffic spikes; over/under-provisioning risk

top_gaps.title: Backup and Recovery
top_gaps.gap: RDS has no backup_retention_period configured (defaults to 0); no backup plans
top_gaps.impact: Data loss risk with no recovery capability

top_gaps.title: Anomaly Detection and Alerting
top_gaps.gap: No alerting or anomaly detection configured
top_gaps.impact: Degradation goes undetected until user reports

top_gaps.title: Encryption at Rest
top_gaps.gap: No storage_encrypted on RDS; no KMS keys configured
top_gaps.impact: Sensitive user data stored unencrypted at rest

top_gaps.title: Incident Response Automation
top_gaps.gap: No runbooks, incident response docs, or automated remediation
top_gaps.impact: Mean-time-to-recovery entirely dependent on individual knowledge

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 2; GitHub Actions workflows with build, test, and deploy stages)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that triggers deployments, checks build status, monitors CI pipeline health, and manages releases via GitHub Actions API
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: GitHub Actions REST API access needs to be configured; deploy workflow could expose webhook triggers

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists in repo (README, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md detected during discovery)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A knowledge agent using existing documentation as a corpus to answer developer questions about ToolJet architecture, deployment, and plugin development
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Documentation needs to be indexed; existing docs/ directory and contributor guides provide the corpus

quick_agent_wins.name: Observability Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Structured logging and tracing in place (OPS-Q1 = 3; comprehensive OpenTelemetry with traces, metrics, custom business metrics)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that queries OTEL traces and metrics, correlates incidents across service boundaries, and suggests root causes based on trace patterns
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: OTEL collector endpoint needs to be accessible to the agent; custom metrics provide rich signal

decomposition_strategy.rationale: ToolJet's 58 NestJS modules provide clear boundary candidates for extraction. Modular monolith structure with distinct module directories makes incremental extraction feasible.
priority_extraction_candidates: AI Module - Self-contained, rapidly evolving, distinct compute profile
Workflow Module - Already designed for external orchestration (Temporal)
Data-Queries Module - High fan-out to 50+ data sources; independent scaling needs

effort_factors.data_coupling: High effort - shared PostgreSQL with cross-module access
effort_factors.stored_procedures: Low effort - business logic in application layer
effort_factors.communication_patterns: Medium effort - need to externalize inter-module calls
effort_factors.cicd_maturity: Medium effort - pipeline needs multi-service support
effort_factors.test_coverage: Medium effort - 42 e2e tests provide baseline

findings.title: No Auto-Scaling Configured
findings.description: No auto-scaling configured in ECS Terraform (static desired_count = 2). Helm HPA exists but defaults to min=1, max=1 (effectively disabled). No Application Auto Scaling for ECS.
findings.gap: All capacity is statically provisioned. No auto-scaling on compute, database, or caching layers. System cannot respond to traffic changes.
findings.recommendation: Add ECS Application Auto Scaling with target tracking on CPU/memory and custom metrics. Enable HPA in Helm with appropriate min/max. Consider Aurora Auto Scaling for read replicas.

findings.title: No Backup and Recovery Configuration
findings.description: RDS instance has no backup_retention_period configured (defaults to 0 = no automated backups). No aws_backup_plan resources. skip_final_snapshot = true means no snapshot on deletion.
findings.gap: Zero backup and recovery capability. Data loss on any failure is permanent. No restore procedures.
findings.recommendation: Configure backup_retention_period = 7 (minimum) on RDS with PITR enabled. Add aws_backup_plan for cross-service backup orchestration. Enable Redis persistence or migrate to ElastiCache with automated backups.

findings.title: Single-AZ Database Without Fault Isolation
findings.description: ECS spans 2 subnets in different AZs for compute, but RDS is single-AZ (no multi_az = true). Redis runs as a sidecar with no replication. AMI/EC2 deployment is single-AZ.
findings.gap: Database (RDS) is single-AZ — an AZ failure takes down the entire data layer. Redis sidecar has no failover.
findings.recommendation: Enable multi_az = true on RDS. Migrate Redis to ElastiCache with Multi-AZ replication. Remove single-AZ EC2 deployment path or add ASG with multi-AZ.

findings.title: No Encryption at Rest
findings.description: RDS instance has no storage_encrypted parameter configured. No KMS keys defined in any Terraform configuration. EBS volumes on EC2 have no encryption.
findings.gap: All data at rest is unencrypted — PostgreSQL database, Redis data, and EBS volumes. No KMS key management.
findings.recommendation: Add storage_encrypted = true with a customer-managed KMS key on RDS. Configure EBS encryption by default. Migrate Redis to ElastiCache with at-rest encryption.

findings.title: No SLO Definitions
findings.description: No SLO definitions found anywhere in the repository. No error budget tracking. No formal definition of acceptable service levels.
findings.gap: No SLOs — system reliability has no formal measurement or target.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for critical user journeys: app loading time (p99 < 3s), API response time (p95 < 500ms), availability (99.9%). Implement error budget tracking.

findings.title: No Anomaly Detection or Alerting
findings.description: No alerting or anomaly detection configured in IaC. No CloudWatch alarms, no PagerDuty/OpsGenie integration. OTEL exports metrics but no consumers are defined.
findings.gap: No alerting of any kind — system degradation goes undetected until user reports.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch alarms for API error rate, p99 latency, concurrent users anomaly, ECS task health. Integrate with SNS for on-call alerting. Enable CloudWatch anomaly detection.

findings.title: No Incident Response Automation
findings.description: No runbooks, incident response documentation, or automated remediation found. No Systems Manager Automation documents. No self-healing patterns.
findings.gap: Incident response is entirely ad hoc. No documented procedures for common failure scenarios.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for top failure scenarios. Implement SSM Automation documents for common remediations. Add Lambda-based auto-scaling triggers for capacity incidents.

findings.title: Partially Managed Databases with Self-Managed Components
findings.description: Production ECS uses RDS PostgreSQL 16 (managed) but Redis 6.2 runs as a self-managed sidecar. RDS is single-AZ without Multi-AZ failover enabled.
findings.gap: Redis is self-managed as a sidecar. Production RDS is single-AZ without Multi-AZ failover.
findings.recommendation: Migrate Redis to Amazon ElastiCache or MemoryDB with Multi-AZ. Enable Multi-AZ on RDS. Consider Aurora PostgreSQL for automated failover and enhanced performance.

findings.title: Network Security Gaps - Public Subnets Only
findings.description: ECS Terraform creates a VPC with 2 public subnets only. No private subnets. AMI/EC2 deployment puts instances directly in public subnet with public IP.
findings.gap: No private subnets — ECS tasks and RDS are in public subnets. No VPC endpoints or PrivateLink.
findings.recommendation: Restructure VPC with public/private subnet tiers. Place ECS tasks and RDS in private subnets. Add VPC endpoints for AWS services.

findings.title: API Entry Point Lacks Throttling and Auth
findings.description: ALB is configured with health checks. No throttling, request validation, or authentication at the gateway level.
findings.gap: ALB provides basic routing only. No API Gateway with throttling, auth, or request validation.
findings.recommendation: Add Amazon API Gateway in front of the ALB for throttling, request validation, and centralized auth. Configure WAF rules for rate limiting.

findings.title: Partial Infrastructure as Code Coverage
findings.description: Terraform covers ECS, VPC, ALB, RDS, and security groups. No IaC for CloudWatch alarms, Route 53, backup plans, WAF, or operational resources.
findings.gap: IaC covers compute and networking but not operational/DR resources. No shared Terraform modules.
findings.recommendation: Extend IaC to cover CloudWatch alarms, backup plans, and WAF rules. Create shared Terraform modules. Add default_tags to AWS provider.

findings.title: Partial CI/CD - Manual Deployment
findings.description: GitHub Actions provides automated build and tests. Deployment requires manual workflow trigger. No automated rollback. No IaC deployment pipeline.
findings.gap: Build is automated but deploy is manual. No continuous deployment. No IaC pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Implement continuous deployment: build → test → deploy-staging → integration-test → promote-to-prod. Add Terraform plan/apply in CI. Implement automated rollback.

findings.title: Secrets in Environment Variables Without Rotation
findings.description: Production credentials passed via environment variables. ECS has Secrets Manager IAM policy but no explicit aws_secretsmanager_secret resources. No automated rotation.
findings.gap: Credentials in env vars without rotation. Kubernetes Secrets are base64-encoded. No rotation configured.
findings.recommendation: Define aws_secretsmanager_secret resources in Terraform. Configure automatic rotation for DB credentials. Reference secrets from Secrets Manager in ECS task definition.

findings.title: Limited Compute Hardening
findings.description: ECS Fargate eliminates OS patching. Dependabot and Grype configured. No hardened base images for EC2 path. No AWS Inspector.
findings.gap: EC2/AMI path uses unhardened custom AMI. Vulnerability scanning is periodic rather than blocking.
findings.recommendation: Use Bottlerocket for container hosts. Enable AWS Inspector. Make vulnerability scanning a blocking CI gate.

findings.title: No SAST in Security Pipeline
findings.description: Dependency scanning via npm audit and Grype exists. No SAST tool (SonarQube, Semgrep). Scanning is weekly/advisory, not blocking.
findings.gap: No SAST for source code analysis. Vulnerability scanning not per-commit. No blocking gate.
findings.recommendation: Add SAST (Semgrep or SonarQube) to CI on every PR. Make npm audit a blocking step. Integrate ECR image scanning with blocking on critical findings.

findings.title: Modular Monolith with Shared Database
findings.description: 58 NestJS modules in a single deployable unit. Clear module structure but shared PostgreSQL with cross-module entity access.
findings.gap: Shared database schemas with cross-module access. Single deployable unit prevents independent scaling.
findings.recommendation: Begin Strangler Fig decomposition starting with AI module and data-queries module. Introduce per-module schemas.

findings.title: Infrastructure Metrics Only
findings.description: Custom OTEL metrics include users.concurrent and sessions.active. No metrics for core business outcomes (apps created, queries executed, deployments).
findings.gap: Metrics are infrastructure-focused with minimal business context. No business KPI dashboards.
findings.recommendation: Add business metrics: apps_created, queries_executed, active_workspaces, workflow_executions. Build dashboards tracking business outcomes.

findings.title: No Observability Ownership Model
findings.description: CODEOWNERS covers package.json/module.ts only. No observability assets owned. No per-service dashboards. No alarm ownership.
findings.gap: No observability ownership model. Monitoring responsibility is undefined.
findings.recommendation: Extend CODEOWNERS to observability configs. Define per-module SLO owners. Create dashboards-as-code with team tags.

findings.title: No Resource Tagging Governance
findings.description: Only Name tags on EC2 resources. ECS Terraform has zero tags. No default_tags. No tagging standard.
findings.gap: Cannot track costs, identify ownership, or enforce budgets. No tagging governance.
findings.recommendation: Add default_tags block to AWS provider. Implement required-tags Config rule. Enable cost allocation tags.

findings.title: Mixed Compute with EC2 Paths Remaining
findings.description: Primary production uses ECS Fargate. EC2/AMI deployment options remain as alternative paths.
findings.gap: EC2/AMI paths alongside managed compute. Not all deployment paths use managed orchestration.
findings.recommendation: Deprecate EC2/AMI deployment paths; standardize on EKS or ECS Fargate.

findings.title: Workflow Orchestration Partial - EE Only
findings.description: Temporal.io integrated for EE. BullMQ handles background jobs. Community Edition lacks dedicated workflow orchestration.
findings.gap: Temporal is EE-only. CE has no workflow orchestration for multi-step operations.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate AWS Step Functions as managed alternative to Temporal for all editions.

findings.title: Async Messaging via Redis-Backed BullMQ Only
findings.description: BullMQ provides async job processing. No managed AWS messaging (SQS, SNS, EventBridge). If Redis fails, all async processing stops.
findings.gap: No managed messaging service. Redis is single point of failure for async processing.
findings.recommendation: Adopt Amazon SQS for job queues and EventBridge for event-driven patterns as decomposition progresses.

findings.title: Environment Variable Based Service Discovery
findings.description: Services use environment variables (TOOLJET_HOST, PG_HOST, REDIS_HOST). No dynamic discovery. ECS uses localhost for sidecars.
findings.gap: All endpoints are env-var-based. No dynamic service discovery.
findings.recommendation: Implement AWS Cloud Map for service discovery as decomposition progresses.

findings.title: No S3 Infrastructure for Unstructured Data
findings.description: File storage configuration is environment-variable-driven. No S3 bucket defined in Terraform IaC. Textract plugin exists but not in a cohesive pipeline.
findings.gap: No S3 infrastructure in IaC. No managed object storage pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Define S3 buckets in Terraform for assets, uploads, and exports. Establish document processing pipeline with Textract plugin.

findings.title: Rolling Update Only - No Staged Rollout
findings.description: Kubernetes uses RollingUpdate. ECS has basic health check. No canary, blue/green, or traffic shifting.
findings.gap: No staged rollout or automated rollback on error rate increase.
findings.recommendation: Implement blue/green via AWS CodeDeploy or Argo Rollouts. Add automated rollback triggered by CloudWatch alarm.

findings.title: Integration Tests Not in Deployment Pipeline
findings.description: 42 e2e tests and Cypress suites exist. CI runs unit tests. Cypress E2E not run as part of deployment pipeline.
findings.gap: Integration tests exist but not consistently run on every deployment. No contract tests.
findings.recommendation: Integrate Cypress E2E into deployment pipeline as gate. Add contract tests for inter-module interfaces.

evaluations.reason: Application-level IaC only; CloudTrail is account-level

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2=2 (monolith with shared DB) AND INF-Q1=3 (partial managed compute)

triggering_questions.note: Modular monolith with shared database schemas
Mixed compute with EC2 paths remaining

immediate_actions: Identify service boundaries for AI and workflow modules
Implement Anti-corruption Layer for first extraction

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=3 and container definitions already exist (16 Dockerfiles, Helm chart, Kubernetes manifests). Compute is already containerized on ECS Fargate.

triggering_questions.note: Already on ECS Fargate; contextual guard prevents trigger

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Workload already runs on ECS Fargate with Dockerfiles and Helm chart. Container pathway does not apply.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=3 (minimal stored procedures). PostgreSQL is already open source. No commercial DB engines detected.

triggering_questions.note: Score meets threshold; no commercial engines detected

pathways.not_triggered_reason: PostgreSQL and Redis are already open-source engines. No commercial database products detected.

triggering_questions.note: Redis self-managed as sidecar; RDS single-AZ without Multi-AZ

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads, ETL, streaming, or analytics infrastructure detected in repository.

triggering_questions.note: Score meets threshold; no data processing workloads exist

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No streaming, ETL, or analytics artifacts found. Application is a CRUD platform without data processing responsibilities.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10=2 (partial IaC), INF-Q11=2 (manual deployment), OPS-Q5=2 (rolling update only), OPS-Q6=2 (integration tests not in deploy pipeline)

triggering_questions.note: IaC covers compute/networking but not operational resources
Rolling update only, no staged rollout
Integration tests exist but not in deploy pipeline

immediate_actions: Add Terraform plan/apply to CI pipeline
Integrate Cypress E2E as deployment gate
Extend IaC to cover alarms and backup plans

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: AI/agent frameworks already present: AWS Bedrock plugin, OpenAI SDK, Anthropic plugin, server AI module with graph/agent services, vector DB plugins (Pinecone, Qdrant, Weaviate).
pathways.not_triggered_reason: AI/agent frameworks are already integrated. The server has a dedicated AI module and marketplace includes Bedrock, OpenAI, Anthropic, vector DB plugins.
